key_insight: Conforming finite-element matrices often admit an element-local Gram/SPSD structure, enabling a black-box multilevel algebraic preconditioner (LS-AMG-DD) built directly from local energy factors.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical, solver-agnostic preconditioning path for hard SPD FEM systems in H(div), H^2, and vector H^1 settings where classical AMG can fail or become prohibitively slow.
method: Identify a local Gram representation A = G^T G from element-wise SPSD energies on a chosen DOF cover.
Build local spectral problems from the SPSD splitting to construct interpolation P.
Form the coarse operator algebraically via G_c = G P and A_c = G_c^T G_c under Galerkin coarsening.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports robust performance on challenging problems where classical AMG required more than 10^5 iterations, with LS-AMG-DD sometimes achieving 2–5 orders of magnitude smaller errors at the same residual tolerance.
watch_item: The approach depends on finding a suitable local factorization/Gram structure for each element block, so implementation quality and cover choice may affect robustness and cost.
paper_title: A black-box, multilevel algebraic preconditioning framework for conforming finite elements
